title: ThreadDataQuery Object
description: This object provides methods to query the thread data contained in the XML files in ThreadData folder within the Fusion install folder. You can use the queried values to create a ThreadInfo object that is then used to create a thread feature.

methods.name: allClasses
methods.description: Returns and array/list of all the available classes for a thread type of a given thread designation.

methods.name: allDesignations
methods.description: returns an array/list of all the available thread designations for a thread type of a given size. Valid thread types and sizes and be obtained by using the allThreadTypes and allSizes functions.

methods.name: allSizes
methods.description: Returns an array/list of all the available thread sizes for a given thread type. You can use the allThreadTypes property to get the available thread types.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: create
methods.description: Static method to create a new ThreadDataQuery object. The ThreadDataQuery object is a utility object that provides methods to query for the valid thread definitions defined in Fusion. This object provides similar functionality as the Thread and Hole command dialogs to find valid thread types, designations and classes which can be used to create thread and tapped hole features.

methods.name: recommendThreadData
methods.description: Method that gets the recommended thread data for a given cylinder diameter. This method is only valid for straight threads and will fail for tapered threads.

methods.name: threadTypeCustomName
methods.description: Method that returns the custom name for a given thread type. The custom name is the localized name of the thread type using the current language specified for Fusion.

methods.name: threadTypeUnit
methods.description: Method that returns the unit for a given thread type.

properties.name: allThreadTypes
properties.description: This method returns an array of all the available thread types (families). The type names are always English. This English name should be used in the other methods that take the type as an input argument. If you need to display the type name to the user, you can use the threadTypeCustomName method To get the localized name.

properties.name: defaultInchThreadType
properties.description: Gets the default thread type for inch threads.

properties.name: defaultMetricThreadType
properties.description: Gets the default thread type for metric threads.

properties.name: isTapered
properties.description: Returns if this ThreadDataQuery was created to query for standard or tapered threads.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Thread Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new thread feature.

allClasses.title: ThreadDataQuery.allClasses Method
allClasses.description: Returns and array/list of all the available classes for a thread type of a given thread designation.
allClasses.return_description: Returns the specified thread classes or empty array/list if an invalid thread type or designation was specified.

parameters.name: isInternal
parameters.description: Indicates if the thread is an internal or external thread.

parameters.name: threadType
parameters.description: The thread type of the thread class you want.

parameters.name: designation
parameters.description: The thread designation of the thread class you want.

related_samples.name: Thread Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new thread feature.

allDesignations.title: ThreadDataQuery.allDesignations Method
allDesignations.description: returns an array/list of all the available thread designations for a thread type of a given size. Valid thread types and sizes and be obtained by using the allThreadTypes and allSizes functions.
allDesignations.return_description: Returns the specified thread designations or empty array/list if an invalid thread type or size was specified.

parameters.name: threadType
parameters.description: The thread type of the designation you want.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The thread size of the designation you want.

related_samples.name: Thread Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new thread feature.

allSizes.title: ThreadDataQuery.allSizes Method
allSizes.description: Returns an array/list of all the available thread sizes for a given thread type. You can use the allThreadTypes property to get the available thread types.
allSizes.return_description: Returns the specified thread sizes or an empty array/list if an invalid thread type was specified.

related_samples.name: Thread Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new thread feature.

classType.title: ThreadDataQuery.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

create.title: ThreadDataQuery.create Method
create.description: Static method to create a new ThreadDataQuery object. The ThreadDataQuery object is a utility object that provides methods to query for the valid thread definitions defined in Fusion. This object provides similar functionality as the Thread and Hole command dialogs to find valid thread types, designations and classes which can be used to create thread and tapped hole features.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = adsk.fusion.ThreadDataQuery.create()

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = adsk.fusion.ThreadDataQuery.create(isTapered)

parameters.name: isTapered
parameters.description: Specifies if you want to query for standard or tapered holes.   This is an optional argument whose default value is False.

recommendThreadData.title: ThreadDataQuery.recommendThreadData Method
recommendThreadData.description: Method that gets the recommended thread data for a given cylinder diameter. This method is only valid for straight threads and will fail for tapered threads.

parameters.name: modelDiameter
parameters.description: The diameter of the cylinder the thread will be placed on. The units are centimeters.

parameters.name: isInternal
parameters.description: Indicates if the thread is an internal or external thread.

parameters.name: threadType
parameters.description: Specifies the thread type to query the thread data.

threadTypeCustomName.title: ThreadDataQuery.threadTypeCustomName Method
threadTypeCustomName.description: Method that returns the custom name for a given thread type. The custom name is the localized name of the thread type using the current language specified for Fusion.
threadTypeCustomName.return_description: Returns the specified custom name or an empty string if an invalid thread type was specified.

parameters.name: threadType
parameters.description: The thread type you want to get the custom name for.

threadTypeUnit.title: ThreadDataQuery.threadTypeUnit Method
threadTypeUnit.description: Method that returns the unit for a given thread type.
threadTypeUnit.return_description: Returns the specified unit or empty string if an invalid thread type was specified.

parameters.name: threadType
parameters.description: The thread type you want to get the thread unit type for.

allThreadTypes.title: ThreadDataQuery.allThreadTypes Property
allThreadTypes.description: This method returns an array of all the available thread types (families). The type names are always English. This English name should be used in the other methods that take the type as an input argument. If you need to display the type name to the user, you can use the threadTypeCustomName method To get the localized name.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = threadDataQuery_var.allThreadTypes
